task_description:
# Catholic Content Management Platform – PRD

### TL;DR

A personal platform for Ramir to manage the full lifecycle of Catholic content — from AI-assisted generation (Claude, ChatGPT) to theological validation via NotebookLM, editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It specifically supports short-form video scripts (1-min), long-form video scripts (10-min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese, ensuring both speed and doctrinal accuracy for a Catholic content creator.

---

## Goals

### Business Goals

* Centralize all Catholic content production activities in a single platform.
* Reduce average time-to-publish for each piece of content by at least 50%.
* Increase consistency in publishing, achieving a minimum of 3 content pieces per week.
* Ensure 100% of published content is validated for theological accuracy.
* Minimize post-publication corrections by implementing robust doctrinal review.

### User Goals

* Rapidly generate content using AI while maintaining a uniquely Catholic voice.
* Validate content against authoritative Church sources (without building custom RAG pipelines).
* Organize content into an accessible editorial calendar with clear status tracking.
* Schedule delivery for each piece on appropriate channels (e.g., YouTube, Blog).
* Easily curate, update, and archive content with full version and validation history.

### Non-Goals

* Building or maintaining a custom Catholic knowledge base (handled entirely by NotebookLM).
* Undertaking broad social media management (e.g., analytics, community interaction) beyond basic scheduling/delivery reminders.
* Implementing monetization or revenue-generating features.

---

## User Stories

**Persona:** Ramir, Catholic Content Creator

* As Ramir, I want to create a new short-form video script using Claude or ChatGPT, so that I can generate engaging 1-min video content quickly.
* As Ramir, I want to validate my script against the full Catholic corpus available in NotebookLM, so that I am confident my content aligns with Church teaching.
* As Ramir, I want to schedule my validated content on an editorial calendar, so that I maintain a consistent publishing cadence across channels.
* As Ramir, I want to curate and edit blog posts for SEO (in Portuguese), so that my written content reaches a larger audience via search engines.
* As Ramir, I want to review the current status (draft, validated, scheduled, published) and destination (YouTube, Blog, etc.) of all my content, so that I’m always aware of what’s next.

---

## Functional Requirements

* **Content Creation (Priority: High)**

  * AI Drafting Integration: Connect to Claude and ChatGPT APIs for rapid content generation.
  * Content Type Templates: Pre-configured templates for short-form video, long-form video, and blog posts.

* **Theological Validation (Priority: High)**

  * NotebookLM Integration: Use a third-party library for doctrinal source validation.
  * Validation Score/Flag System: Visual feedback indicating theological alignment.
  * Source Citation Display: Display relevant citations from the Catholic corpus.

* **Editorial Calendar (Priority: Medium)**

  * Scheduling by Content Type and Channel: Assign publication dates and platforms.
  * Status Tracking: Transparently manage content states (draft, validated, scheduled, published).

* **SEO & Curation Tools (Priority: Medium)**

  * Portuguese SEO Metadata: Fields and checks for keywords, meta, and descriptions in Portuguese.
  * Readability Checks: Automated assessment of text clarity.
  * Keyword Suggestions: AI-powered prompts for blog post optimization.

* **Channel Management (Priority: Low)**

  * Channel Tagging: Associate content with YouTube, Blog, or other platforms.
  * Delivery Reminders: Notify when content is due to be published or reviewed.

---

## User Experience

**Entry Point & First-Time User Experience**

* User logs in and lands on a dashboard summarizing the content pipeline, showing draft/validated/scheduled/published items, and the editorial calendar.
* Optional onboarding walkthrough highlights how to create content, trigger validation, and use the editor.

**Core Experience**

* **Step 1:** Create a new content item.
  * Choose content type: short video, long video, or blog post.
  * Select language preference (default: Portuguese for blog).
  * Clear, minimal form – no unnecessary friction.
* **Step 2:** AI generation panel.
  * Choose AI model: Claude or ChatGPT.
  * Enter topic/prompt and generate draft.
  * Validate prompt entry; handle API errors gracefully.
  * Show generated draft in main editor.
* **Step 3:** Trigger NotebookLM validation.
  * One-click validation sends content to third-party NotebookLM library.
  * Return and display validation score/flags and doctrinal citations.
  * Validation panel anchored on the right side of the editor.
* **Step 4:** Content curation and editing.
  * Edit draft based on validation results.
  * Option to view citation sources directly.
  * Save drafts and track validation history.
* **Step 5:** Assign to channel and schedule.
  * Tag content to delivery platform.
  * Choose publication date via editorial calendar UI.
  * Status updates to “Scheduled.”
* **Step 6:** Mark as published and archive.
  * Mark content as published after delivery.
  * Optionally archive for future reference and analytics.

**Advanced Features & Edge Cases**

* Bulk scheduling: Assign dates and channels for multiple items at once.
* Validation history: View prior validation runs and responses.
* Content versioning: Track major edits and revert if necessary.
* Handle validation or AI API downtime gracefully (clear errors, retry options).

**UI/UX Highlights**

* Single-column editor central to workflow; validation feedback panel fixed to the right.
* Editorial calendar as a visual week/month view; click-in to schedule or review items.
* Portuguese-first interface and localization for SEO/blog workflows.
* Consistent, accessible color palette and responsive design for all device types.

---

## Narrative

Ramir sits down at his desk on a Tuesday morning with inspiration to create a 1-minute video script about the upcoming Feast of Corpus Christi. He opens his Catholic Content Management Platform and immediately sees his dashboard, which highlights content drafts and this week’s publishing schedule. Selecting the “short video” template, he enters a brief prompt for Claude, who quickly generates a concise, engaging script in less than a minute.

Before moving forward, Ramir clicks the “Validate with NotebookLM” button. Within seconds, the platform provides a score indicating strong doctrinal support but flags two statements for clarification. The tool seamlessly surfaces source citations from the Catechism and Vatican II documents, making it easy for Ramir to reference and refine his wording to ensure precise Catholic teaching.

Satisfied with the script, he assigns it to his short-video channel and schedules it for Thursday’s publication. Motivated by the smooth workflow, he turns to the blog post section—writes, validates, optimizes SEO in Portuguese, and schedules another article, all in one productive sitting. Ramir knows that every piece he publishes aligns with Church authority, publishes on time, and is organized for maximum reach—giving him both confidence and creative momentum.

---

## Success Metrics

### User-Centric Metrics

* Average time (in minutes) from content idea to scheduled item (tracked via timestamps).
* Number of validation cycles per content piece before approval.
* Number of content pieces published per week.
* User feedback on validation accuracy and workflow satisfaction.

### Business Metrics

* Consistency of weekly publishing cadence (minimum 3 items/week).
* Reduction in after-publication corrections or removals due to doctrinal issues.
* Adoption/continued use rate (e.g., items managed per week/month).

### Technical Metrics

* Average response time from NotebookLM API (<2 seconds per call).
* Average AI generation latency (<5 seconds per request).
* Content editor uptime (>99% during weekday business hours).
* Robust error-rate tracking on API integrations (<2% failure rate).

### Tracking Plan

* Content item created (event)
* AI generation triggered (event, with selected model)
* Validation initiated/completed (event, with score/result)
* Content scheduled (event, with channel/date)
* Marked as published (event)
* User edits/re-validation cycles (event)

---

## Technical Considerations

### Technical Needs

* Web application frontend with content editor, dashboard, and calendar views.
* API integrations for Anthropic (Claude), OpenAI (ChatGPT), and third-party NotebookLM library.
* Lightweight backend for routing requests, managing validation/scheduling logic, and storing user data.
* Database for persistent storage of content items, validation responses, scheduling metadata, and channel assignments.

### Integration Points

* Anthropic Claude API for content drafting.
* OpenAI ChatGPT API for alternate drafting option.
* NotebookLM third-party library for source validation and citation retrieval.
* Option for editorial calendar export (iCal or Google Calendar integration for reminders).

### Data Storage & Privacy

* All data (drafts, validation results, scheduling info) stored privately and securely for Ramir only.
* Ensure encryption at rest and in transit.
* No personal data shared externally or sold.
* Compliance with GDPR and local privacy standards as relevant.

### Scalability & Performance

* Designed for single-user, low daily volume (tens of content items per week).
* Minimal scaling risk.
* Prioritize fast response for workflow efficiency.

### Potential Challenges

* Reliability and rate limits of NotebookLM’s third-party library/API.
* Maintaining consistent, high-quality Portuguese output from AI models (especially for SEO).
* Managing failing API calls or downtime from AI or validation services.
* Ensuring third-party library continues to track authoritative Catholic sources.

---

## Milestones & Sequencing

### Project Estimate

* Medium: 3–4 weeks total build time.

### Team Size & Composition

* Small Team: 1–2 people (Ramir + 1 developer, or Ramir with AI-assisted development).

### Suggested Phases

**Phase 1: Core Content Creation (Week 1–2)**

* Key Deliverables: Developer sets up the platform, builds content editor, configures templates (short/long video, blog post), and integrates Claude/ChatGPT APIs.
* Dependencies: Claude/OpenAI API keys.

**Phase 2: Theological Validation Integration (Week 2–3)**

* Key Deliverables: Developer integrates NotebookLM via third-party library, implements validation feedback panel with score/flag/citations, error handling for API.
* Dependencies: NotebookLM third-party library.

**Phase 3: Editorial Calendar & Channel Management (Week 3–4)**

* Key Deliverables: Develop editorial calendar UI, scheduling system, channel tagging, delivery reminders, SEO metadata tools for Portuguese blogs.
* Dependencies: Calendar integration (if needed), basic notification system.

---
